exam_title: IBPS PO MAINS 2018 ENGLISH LANGUAGE MEMORY BASED PAPER
subject: English Language

questions.question: Which of the following can be inferred as the theme of the passage?

options.a: Certificate of deposits is the best way to establish savings habit.
options.b: Establishing a consistent saving habit and also smart saving with CDs.
options.c: Employer's 401(k) plan provides a convenient way for consistent and smart savings.
options.d: Your savings decide how well you flourish and grow.

questions.question: Which of the following can replace the word given in bold in the passage?
questions.note: Context: If your employer matches part of your contribution... you will accumulate yet more.

questions.question: What is the tone of writing in the passage?
Which of the following words can fill in the blank to make it meaningful?
questions.note: Context: CDs usually offer higher interest rates, but they are time deposits and have ____ for early withdrawal.

questions.question: Which of the followings is the correct chronology of steps as can be inferred from the passage?
Which of the following words, opposite of the word given in bold, should replace the word given in bold in the passage?
questions.note: Context: Try to figure out ways in which you can increase [bolded] expenses.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best way of writing the line given in italics in the paragraph, without changing its intended meaning?
questions.note: Italics line: Ensure that your income exceeds the expenses with as wider a margin as possible.

options.a: Surely, the income will exceed the expenses by a wider margin.
options.b: The income will be greater than the expenses is not a possibility.
options.c: Make sure that the income is greater than the expenses by a margin as wide as possible.
options.d: If the income does not exceed the expenses by a wider margin, it will be useless.

questions.question: Which of the following can be best inferred from the paragraph?

options.a: The better way to save is to give due consideration to current and future needs and plan accordingly.
options.b: It's better to buy the things that you require at present than to save money and buy them later.
options.c: Buying a watch is more important than saving for the future.
options.d: A stringent savings plan will produce a good result for the future.

questions.question: Which of the following word should fill in the blank in the paragraph?
questions.note: Context: So the ____ way to buy a coveted watch is not to swipe the credit card...

questions.question: Which of the following can be next line after the last line of the given paragraph?

options.a: Most of us never have enough money for immediate expenses as well as saving.
options.b: There will be a lot of unwanted expenses that can creep in every now and then.
options.c: If we had enough money to meet our current expenses and future goals, there would be no problem.
options.d: Expenses or needs that have to be met at a point of time in the future are called goals.
options.e: Just as we segregate the essential and less important things in our shopping list, do the same with your goals.

questions.question: Which of the following assumptions could be drawn out most appropriately from a clause in the first paragraph 'Pakistan's Army and political leadership are on "one page" to take dialogue with India forward'?
What does Mr. Khan mean by referring to the Kashmir dispute as the "single issue" between India and Pakistan?

options.a: According to Mr. Khan, there is only one issue between India and Pakistan which needs to be solved.
options.b: According to Mr. Khan, dispute on Kashmir is an issue among other important unresolved issues between India and Pakistan which has the highest importance.
options.c: According to Mr. Khan, Kashmir is a part of both India and Pakistan.
options.d: According to Mr. Khan, India and Pakistan have failed to resolve the issue between India and Pakistan.

questions.question: Which of the following words has the meaning which is SIMILAR to the meaning 'discordant'?
Which of the followings, as mentioned in the passage, could create hurdles for the government in its desire to intervene and convince Indigo and SpiceJet to withdraw web check-in fee?

options.a: Ministry of Civil Aviation or government has no authority to give directions to airlines.
options.b: Airlines can unbundle air-fare and charges for services.
options.c: Making airlines to withdraw web check-in fees would invite protest from them and they would stop operating their aircrafts.

questions.question: Which of the following statements, if true, would encourage the government to convince Indigo and SpiceJet to withdraw their web check-in fee?
Which of the following words has a meaning which is OPPOSITE in meaning to the word 'withdraw'?
Why did the Delhi High Court use the phrase 'Dark Chapter in the history of Independent India'?

options.a: The 1984 anti-Sikh riots were not religiously motivated riots.
options.b: The 1984 anti-Sikh riots led to the defense of innocent people belonging to several communities
options.c: The 1984 anti-Sikh riots were shameful events in the history of independent India.

questions.question: What is the opinion of the Delhi High Court about the manner of prosecution of the 1984 anti-Sikh riots?

options.a: The manner of prosecution of the 1984 anti-Sikh riots has been effective in delivering justice.
options.b: The manner of prosecution of the 1984 anti-Sikh riots has been very pathetic and must not be repeated in the Indian Judiciary.
options.c: The way prosecution of the 1984 anti-Sikh riots was conducted provided timely justice to the victims.

questions.question: Which of the following words has a meaning which is SIMILAR in meaning to the word 'absconded'?
Which of the followings judges believe that courts have failed to uphold the principle of justice?

options.a: The given passage doesn't talk if courts have failed to uphold the principle of justice.

questions.question: Which of the following words has a meaning which is OPPOSITE in meaning to the word 'disagreed'?
Which of the following word given in the options should come at the place marked as (A) in the above paragraph to make it grammatically correct and meaningful?
In the passage given, a sentence (B) is given in italics. Choose the part which has an error in it.

options.a: A survey of more than 150 council-run schemes by
options.b: Church Action on Poverty found that nearly a quarter
options.c: had been shutted down since 2013, while a further
options.d: quarter have reduced spending by 85% or more.

questions.question: The sentence given in (C) has four words given in bold. Which of the following must replace each other?
Which of the following phrases should fill the blank in (D) to make it contextually correct and meaningful?

options.a: facing the severe impacts caused by the crisis
options.b: including job programmes and childcare, but also transfers to other state programmes,
options.c: which are a cause of severe balance of payments
options.d: so some avoid them by limiting how many people are eligible for welfare in the first place.
options.e: such as a lack of money caused by benefit payment problems

questions.question: Which of the following word given in the options should come at the place marked as E in the above paragraph to make it grammatically correct and meaningful?
A word is given in bold in (F). Choose the word which should replace the word 'abdicate' to make the sentence correct and meaningful.
Which of the following statements can come in between the two sentences in place of (G) and maintain the continuity of the paragraph?

options.a: The dynamic nature of our preferred model allows to estimate...
options.b: Continuing investments in the social sectors have been recognized...
options.c: Local authorities are responsible for using their funds to best meet the needs...
options.d: Together they spent £46m on local welfare last year, compared with a national budget of £172m in 2013-14.
options.e: One million people have been lifted out of absolute poverty since 2010...

questions.question: The sentence given in (H) has four words given in bold. Which of the following must replace each other?
In the passage given, a sentence (I) is given in italics. Choose the part which has an error in it.

options.b: as most councils refuse to give cash payments to clients
options.c: in crisis, choosing instead to offer supermarket
options.d: food vouchers or refer them to food banks.

questions.question: Which of the following words should fill the blank given in (J) to make it contextually correct and meaningful?
Considering statement (D) as the second sentence, which of the following aptly expresses the theme of the paragraph after rearrangement?
Considering statement (D) as the second sentence, then which among the following should be the FOURTH sentence of the rearranged paragraph?
Considering statement (D) as the second sentence, then which among the following should follow the last sentence after the rearrangement?

options.a: They need special processing and packaging to protect them for their required storage life.
options.b: The increasing number of women who now work away from home adds additional pressure for such changes.
options.c: Food has been processed and packaged since the earliest days of man's history on earth.
options.d: The small-scale food processing sector is a major source of employment.

questions.question: Considering statement (D) as the second sentence, then which among the following should coherently link the first and the second sentence of the paragraph?

options.a: The small-scale food processing sector adds value to crops by processing.
options.b: Food and crop processing is generally considered to be the largest industry in most countries.
options.c: The powerful large-scale food sector is also often able to influence government and international policies.
options.d: Food was seldom sold but traded and bartered.
